description: A controller, subscription, or animation created by a State is never disposed, leaking memory and firing on dead widgets.
body: Failure mode: a StatefulWidget's State creates a TextEditingController, AnimationController, StreamSubscription, or Timer but omits the dispose() teardown, so navigating away leaves it alive, growing memory and triggering setState-after-dispose exceptions when callbacks fire on the gone widget. Detection signals: a controller/subscription created in initState with no matching dispose; 'setState() called after dispose()' errors; memory growth across navigation; a ticker still running. Do not create State-owned resources without releasing them. Safer fallback: store each resource on the State, override dispose() to cancel/dispose every one, and add a widget test asserting disposal releases them.
